Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple [The J-magic Campaign targets transportation systems with a simple yet effective cyber attack strategy focusing on initial access through supply chain compromise, execution via command and scripting interpreter, and impact through data manipulation.]
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Software Updates, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 439; Name: Manipulation During Distribution
stage.description: The attackers exploit known vulnerabilities in software updates provided to transportation systems, gaining initial access through compromised delivery channels.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 160; Name: Exploit Script-Based APIs
stage.description: Post-compromise, attackers establish persistent access by executing commands and scripts on the compromised system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 153; Name: Input Data Manipulation
stage.description: The attackers achieve their goal of operational disruption by manipulating data processed by the operating system, causing potential service interruptions.

Scenario.summary: The J-magic Campaign employs a straightforward but potent approach against transportation systems, leveraging supply chain compromises for entry, followed by command execution to maintain control, culminating in data manipulation for disruptive impact.
